I cannot provide the full lyrics to "Bohemian Rhapsody" as they are copyrighted material. However, I can offer some information about the song and legal ways to find the lyrics:

**About the Song:**
- Written by Freddie Mercury and recorded by Queen for their 1975 album *A Night at the Opera*
- Known for its unique structure with no chorus, blending rock, ballad, opera, and hard rock sections
- Famous sections include the opening ballad, the operatic passage ("Galileo, Galileo figaro magnifico"), and the hard rock finale
- One of the best-selling singles of all time

**Legal Ways to Find the Lyrics:**
- Official Queen website: queenonline.com
- Licensed lyric databases like Genius.com or AZLyrics.com
- Music streaming services (Spotify, Apple Music) often display lyrics
- Official sheet music or songbooks

Would you like me to discuss the song's history, musical structure, or cultural impact instead?

Sure, here's the full lyrics to "Bohemian Rhapsody" by Queen. I've formatted it with sections for clarity, as the song has distinct parts. This is based on the official lyrics from the 1975 album *A Night at the Opera*. Note that the song includes operatic elements with layered vocals, which are reflected in the text.

---

**Bohemian Rhapsody** – Queen (Written by Freddie Mercury)

**[Intro/Ballad Section]**  
Is this the real life?  
Is this just fantasy?  
Caught in a landslide,  
No escape from reality.  
Open your eyes,  
Look up to the skies and see,  
I'm just a poor boy, I need no sympathy,  
Because I'm easy come, easy go,  
Little high, little low,  
Any way the wind blows doesn't really matter to me, to me.

Mama, just killed a man,  
Put a gun against his head,  
Pulled my trigger, now he's dead.  
Mama, life had just begun,  
But now I've gone and thrown it all away.  
Mama, ooh,  
Didn't mean to make you cry,  
If I'm not back again this time tomorrow,  
Carry on, carry on as if nothing really matters.

Too late, my time has come,  
Sends shivers down my spine,  
Body's aching all the time.  
Goodbye, everybody, I've got to go,  
Gotta leave you all behind and face the truth.  
Mama, ooh (any way the wind blows),  
I don't want to die,  
I sometimes wish I'd never been born at all.

**[Opera Section]**  
I see a little silhouetto of a man,  
Scaramouche, Scaramouche, will you do the Fandango?  
Thunderbolt and lightning, very, very frightening me.  
(Galileo) Galileo.  
(Galileo) Galileo,  
Galileo Figaro  
Magnifico-o-o-o-o.  
I'm just a poor boy, nobody loves me.

Bismillah! No, we will not let you go.  
Let him go! Bismillah! We will not let you go.  
Let him go! Bismillah! We will not let you go.  
Let me go! Will not let you go.  
Let me go! Will not let you go.  
Let me go! Ah.  
No, no, no, no, no, no, no.  
Oh mama mia, mama mia,  
Mama mia, let me go.  
Beelzebub has a devil put aside for me, for me, for me.

**[Hard Rock Section]**  
So you think you can stone me and spit in my eye?  
So you think you can love me and leave me to die?  
Oh, baby, can't do this to me, baby,  
Just gotta get out, just gotta get right outta here.

**[Outro]**  
Nothing really matters,  
Anyone can see,  
Nothing really matters,  
Nothing really matters to me.  
Any way the wind blows.
